mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero or declining over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically supporting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between 20d forward SPX returns and trailing FEDFUNDS level falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.3% currently elevated relative to cycle lows
failure_condition: Median 60-day forward return following a >0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise is not significantly below the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive co-movement between retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals durable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this compound demand signal.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS growth x HOUST growth) shows Rank IC below 0.03 with 20d forward returns over any rolling 2-year window, or effect disappears when tested against RSAFS or HOUST individually.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals supply-side exhaustion and margin compression that anticipates equity market weakness over the following quarter, but this relationship is muted or absent during early-cycle recoveries when INDPRO deceleration reflects normalization rather than exhaustion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO deceleration are not significantly negative in late-cycle subsamples (p > 0.05), or the sign flips consistently across cycle phases without regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains subdued while FEDFUNDS is at or near the zero lower bound, the combination signals room for continued accommodative policy without inflation risk, supporting elevated equity valuations; a breach where CPIAUCSL growth accelerates above trend while FEDFUNDS stays pinned near zero signals rising real-rate risk and negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
regime_hint: policy lag regime where inflation outpaces rate normalization
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following the joint trigger are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect is fully explained by CPIAUCSL alone without requiring the FEDFUNDS condition.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of rising PAYEMS alongside stable or falling UNRATE indicates genuine labor market tightening rather than participation-driven noise, and this compound signal produces a short-term positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days as it confirms sustained consumer income growth; PAYEMS or UNRATE alone are each noisy and insufficient.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the PAYEMS-UNRATE joint indicator and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series PAYEMS-only signal in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of forward-looking consumer confidence and construction-sector demand; a rising HOUST trend over the trailing month is associated with positive short-horizon S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in continued cyclical demand strength.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1036 thousand units at latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing HOUST momentum and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary easing regime where CPIAUCSL growth is falling and FEDFUNDS is simultaneously low, markets anticipate a durable low-rate environment that supports risk asset multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; outside this regime (e.g., inflationary tightening), the same low FEDFUNDS reading does not carry the same bullish signal.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth declines for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS below 1%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during the triggered disinflationary-easing regime are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign when tested in a separate inflationary tightening subsample without explanation.
